hypothesis: Enforce mandatory proof element in every FAQ answer, including logistics answers (hours, location, shipping, financing). Evidence: exp_7 kept at 8/10 — format generalisation validated but ~3/15 answers dropped proof (Do You Ship Parts, Do You Offer Financing, Where Are You Located). These were the weakest answers — redirect-only, no staff name or specific detail. Hypothesis: adding explicit proof-required rule to FAQ answer structure (every answer must name a staff member OR give a real number/timeline OR cite a specific constraint) will push FAQ format from 8 to 9/10, matching the blog format ceiling. This is the same proof-discipline that pushed exp_3 to 9/10 — applied now at the individual-answer level rather than the lede level.
learning: Proof-in-every-answer rule applied successfully across all 14 answers in seasonal-service-faq.md. Named staff in every section: Lee/Damian/Cody for tech work, Aaron for scheduling and winterisation review, Damian/Lee as handoff CTA for storage, Lynn for parts ID. Even the logistics answers use named staff as handoff (not redirect-only). DIY impeller answer uses operational constraint (lower unit seal failure risk) as proof. Commissioning scope answer uses written-estimate policy — lowest-scoring proof type but still passes. Hypothesis confirmed: mandatory proof rule pushes FAQ format from 8/10 (exp_7) to 9/10 (exp_8), matching blog format ceiling. Rules validated across blog, FAQ, and seasonal FAQ formats at 9/10.
changes_description: Added mandatory proof-in-every-answer rule to FAQ answer structure in current.md. Rule: every FAQ answer (including logistics) must contain named staff OR real number/timeline OR specific local/operational constraint. Addresses the ~3/15 proof-dropout gap identified in exp_7 evaluation (Do You Ship Parts, Do You Offer Financing, Where Are You Located were redirect-only).
